summary: Tested Top-at-Risk widget + GET /api/predictions/fleet/top-risk + URL-driven Device Detail tabs. All backend (8/8 pytest) and frontend (widget + filter chips + recompute + view-all + row deep-link + URL-driven tab switching + default overview) checks passed.

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 200 with {error: 'unknown failure_type'} instead of 400/422. Review spec says 400/error — currently only the 'error' half is honored. Consider raising HTTPException(400) for proper REST semantics.

action_items: Optional: harden GET /predictions/fleet/top-risk to return HTTP 400 (not 200 + error body) for unknown failure_type.
critical_code_review_comments: predictions_fleet.py: unknown failure_type returns 200 with error payload — inconsistent with the single-device endpoint that returns 400. Prefer HTTPException(400).
predictions_fleet.py: `cursor.to_list(2000)` caps at 2000 devices without pagination or fallback logging; fine for now but noted for scale.
DeviceDetailPage.jsx: setActiveTab replaces the URL (replace: true) which prevents back-navigation between tabs — likely intentional but worth confirming with UX.
seed_data_creation: Created two pytest-scoped test devices (fleet-failing with injected failing metrics, fleet-nominal) plus one FleetTestOrg tenant for cross-org isolation test. Existing seeded failing device 722cc76b... continues to be top-ranked as expected.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Widget + fleet endpoint + URL-driven tabs all pass. Injected failing device (722cc76b...) consistently ranks first with 100% probability. If retesting URL-driven tabs, remember setSearchParams uses replace:true (no history entry per tab change).
